Package testutil provides comprehensive tests for validation helpers.\n//\n// These tests demonstrate the usage of request/response validation helpers\n// and ensure they work correctly with the ARMOR test infrastructure.\npackage testutil\n\nimport (\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\t\"time\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// TEST REQUEST BUILDER TESTS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// TestNewTestRequest verifies basic request creation.\nfunc TestNewTestRequest(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname   string\n\t\tmethod string\n\t\tpath   string\n\t\tbody   []byte\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:   \"GET request\",\n\t\t\tmethod: \"GET\",\n\t\t\tpath:   \"/test-bucket/test-key\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:   \"POST request\",\n\t\t\tmethod: \"POST\",\n\t\t\tpath:   \"/test-bucket/test-key\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:   \"PUT request\",\n\t\t\tmethod: \"PUT\",\n\t\t\tpath:   \"/test-bucket/test-key\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:   \"DELETE request\",\n\t\t\tmethod: \"DELETE\",\n\t\t\tpath:   \"/test-bucket/test-key\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\treq := NewTestRequest(tt.method, tt.path, tt.body)\n\n\t\t\tif req.Method != tt.method {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected method %s, got %s\", tt.method, req.Method)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif req.URL.Path != tt.path {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected path %s, got %s\", tt.path, req.URL.Path)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestBuildTestRequest verifies comprehensive request building.\nfunc TestBuildTestRequest(t *testing.T) {\n\tconfig := TestRequestConfig{\n\t\tMethod: \"GET\",\n\t\tPath:   \"/test-bucket/test-key\",\n\t\tHeaders: map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\"X-Custom-Header\": \"custom-value\",\n\t\t\t\"X-Another-Header\": \"another-value\",\n\t\t},\n\t\tQueryParams: map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\"versioning\": \"false\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\treq := BuildTestRequest(config)\n\n\tif req.Method != \"GET\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected method GET, got %s\", req.Method)\n\t}\n\n\tif req.Header.Get(\"X-Custom-Header\") != \"custom-value\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected custom header value\")\n\t}\n\n\tif req.Header.Get(\"X-Another-Header\") != \"another-value\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected another header value\")\n\t}\n\n\tif !strings.Contains(req.URL.RawQuery, \"versioning=false\") {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected query parameter in URL\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestWithAuthHeader verifies authorization header addition.\nfunc TestWithAuthHeader(t *testing.T) {\n\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n\treq = WithAuthHeader(req, \"TESTACCESSKEY\", \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\")\n\n\tauthHeader := req.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n\tif !strings.Contains(authHeader, \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256\") {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 in auth header\")\n\t}\n\n\tif !strings.Contains(authHeader, \"TESTACCESSKEY\") {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected access key in auth header\")\n\t}\n\n\tdateHeader := req.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n\tif dateHeader == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected X-Amz-Date header to be set\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestWithDateHeader verifies date header addition.\nfunc TestWithDateHeader(t *testing.T) {\n\tnow := time.Now()\n\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n\treq = WithDateHeader(req, now)\n\n\tdateHeader := req.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n\texpectedDate := now.Format(\"20060102T150405Z\")\n\n\tif dateHeader != expectedDate {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected date %s, got %s\", expectedDate, dateHeader)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestWithExpiredDate verifies expired date header.\nfunc TestWithExpiredDate(t *testing.T) {\n\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n\treq = WithExpiredDate(req)\n\n\tdateHeader := req.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n\tif dateHeader == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected X-Amz-Date header to be set\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse the date to verify it's in the past\n\tparsedTime, err := time.Parse(\"20060102T150405Z\", dateHeader)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse date header: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif parsedTime.After(time.Now().Add(-15 * time.Minute)) {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected expired date to be at least 15 minutes in the past\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestWithHeader verifies custom header addition.\nfunc TestWithHeader(t *testing.T) {\n\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n\treq = WithHeader(req, \"X-Custom-Header\", \"custom-value\")\n\n\tvalue := req.Header.Get(\"X-Custom-Header\")\n\tif value != \"custom-value\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected header value 'custom-value', got '%s'\", value)\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// REQUEST EXECUTION TESTS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// TestMakeRequest verifies basic request execution.\nfunc TestMakeRequest(t *testing.T) {\n\thandler := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\tw.Write([]byte(\"OK\"))\n\t})\n\n\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test\", nil)\n\tresp := MakeRequest(handler, req)\n\n\tif resp.Code != http.StatusOK {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected status 200, got %d\", resp.Code)\n\t}\n\n\tbody := resp.Body.String()\n\tif body != \"OK\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected body 'OK', got '%s'\", body)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestMakeRequestWithTiming verifies request execution with timing.\nfunc TestMakeRequestWithTiming(t *testing.T) {\n\thandler := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_1338a4664ca9470794ed3a03","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# Generic Test Table Framework\n2\t\n3\t## Overview\n4\t\n5\tThe `testutil` package provides a comprehensive, generic test table framework for writing table-driven tests in Go. Unlike HTTP-specific test frameworks, this framework is designed to work with any type of test case, any input type, and any error type.\n6\t\n7\t## Features\n8\t\n9\t- ✅ **Generic**: Works with any input and error types\n10\t- ✅ **Type-safe**: Strong typing prevents errors at compile time\n11\t- ✅ **Reusable**: Test tables can be shared across multiple test files\n12\t- ✅ **Extensible**: Easy to extend, filter, and manipulate test tables\n13\t- ✅ **Convenient**: Helper functions for common test patterns\n14\t- ✅ **Self-documenting**: Clear field names and comprehensive documentation\n15\t\n16\t## Quick Start\n17\t\n18\t### Basic Example\n19\t\n20\t```go\n21\tfunc TestMyFunction(t *testing.T) {\n22\t    // Define test table\n23\t    table := []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n24\t        {\n25\t            Name:        \"empty string returns error\",\n26\t            Description: \"Tests that empty string returns error\",\n27\t            Input:       \"\",\n28\t            ExpectedError: ErrEmpty,\n29\t            ExpectError:  true,\n30\t        },\n31\t        {\n32\t            Name:        \"valid string succeeds\",\n33\t            Description: \"Tests that valid string succeeds\",\n34\t            Input:       \"valid\",\n35\t            ExpectedError: nil,\n36\t            ExpectError:  false,\n37\t        },\n38\t    }\n39\t\n40\t    // Run test table\n41\t    RunTable(t, table, func(input string) error {\n42\t        return MyFunction(input)\n43\t    })\n44\t}\n45\t```\n46\t\n47\t### Using Helper Functions\n48\t\n49\t```go\n50\tfunc TestMyFunction(t *testing.T) {\n51\t    table := []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n52\t        SuccessCase(\"valid input\", \"test\"),\n53\t        ErrorCase(\"invalid input\", \"\", ErrEmpty),\n54\t        ErrorCaseWithMessage(\"format error\", \"invalid\", ErrInvalidFormat, \"invalid format\"),\n55\t    }\n56\t\n57\t    RunTable(t, table, func(input string) error {\n58\t        return MyFunction(input)\n59\t    })\n60\t}\n61\t```\n62\t\n63\t### Using Table Builder\n64\t\n65\t```go\n66\tfunc TestMyFunction(t *testing.T) {\n67\t    table := NewTableBuilder[string, error]().\n68\t        WithName(\"my tests\").\n69\t        WithDescription(\"Test cases for my function\").\n70\t        WithTags([]string{\"validation\"}).\n71\t        WithTestCase(TableTestCase[string, error]{\n72\t            Name: \"test case\",\n73\t            Input: \"input\",\n74\t            ExpectedError: nil,\n75\t            ExpectError: false,\n76\t        }).\n77\t        Build()\n78\t\n79\t    RunTable(t, table.TestCases, func(input string) error {\n80\t        return MyFunction(input)\n81\t    })\n82\t}\n83\t```\n84\t\n85\t## Core Types\n86\t\n87\t### TableTestCase\n88\t\n89\tRepresents a single test case with the following fields:\n90\t\n91\t- `Name`: Test case name (required)\n92\t- `Description`: Optional detailed description\n93\t- `Input`: The input to test (any type)\n94\t- `ExpectedError`: The expected error\n95\t- `ExpectError`: Whether an error is expected\n96\t- `ErrorContains`: Optional substring for error message validation\n97\t- `ErrorIs`: Optional error for `errors.Is` validation\n98\t- `Skip`: Whether to skip this test case\n99\t- `Tags`: Optional tags for filtering\n100\t\n101\t### TableTestTable\n102\t\n103\tRepresents a collection of test cases:\n104\t\n105\t- `Name`: Table name\n106\t- `Description`: Optional table description\n107\t- `TestCases`: Collection of test cases\n108\t- `Tags`: Optional tags for the entire table\n109\t\n110\t## Helper Functions\n111\t\n112\t### Test Case Creation\n113\t\n114\t- `SuccessCase(name, input)`: Creates a test case expecting success\n115\t- `ErrorCase(name, input, error)`: Creates a test case expecting an error\n116\t- `ErrorCaseWithMessage(name, input, error, message)`: Creates a test case expecting an error with message validation\n117\t\n118\t### Test Execution\n119\t\n120\t- `RunTable(t, table, runner)`: Executes all test cases in a table\n121\t\n122\t### Table Manipulation\n123\t\n124\t- `ExtendTable(base, custom)`: Extends a table with custom test cases\n125\t- `FilterTable(table, tag)`: Filters a table by tag\n126\t- `FilterTableByTag(table, excludeTag)`: Filters a table to exclude tests with a specific tag\n127\t\n128\t### Assertion Helpers\n129\t\n130\t- `AssertNoError(t, err, msg)`: Asserts that no error occurred\n131\t- `AssertError(t, err, msg)`: Asserts that an error occurred\n132\t- `AssertErrorIs(t, err, expected, msg)`: Asserts that the error matches the expected error\n133\t- `AssertErrorContains(t, err, substring, msg)`: Asserts that the error message contains a substring\n134\t\n135\t## Examples\n136\t\n137\t### Example 1: String Validation\n138\t\n139\t```go\n140\tfunc TestStringValidation(t *testing.T) {\n141\t    table := []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n142\t        SuccessCase(\"valid string\", \"test@example.com\"),\n143\t        ErrorCase(\"empty string\", \"\", ErrEmpty),\n144\t        ErrorCaseWithMessage(\"too short\", \"ab\", ErrTooShort, \"at least 3 characters\"),\n145\t        ErrorCaseWithMessage(\"too long\", strings.Repeat(\"a\", 51), ErrTooLong, \"exceed 50\"),\n146\t    }\n147\t\n148\t    RunTable(t, table, ValidateString)\n149\t}\n150\t```\n151\t\n152\t### Example 2: Numeric Validation\n153\t\n154\t```go\n155\tfunc TestNumericValidation(t *testing.T) {\n156\t    table := []TableTestCase[int, error]{\n157\t        {Name: \"negative\", Input: -1, ExpectError: true, ErrorContains: \"non-negative\"},\n158\t        {Name: \"zero\", Input: 0, ExpectError: false},\n159\t        {Name: \"valid\", Input: 50, ExpectError: false},\n160\t        {Name: \"maximum\", Input: 100, ExpectError: false},\n161\t        {Name: \"exceeds max\", Input: 101, ExpectError: true, ErrorContains: \"exceed 100\"},\n162\t    }\n163\t\n164\t    RunTable(t, table, ValidateNumber)\n165\t}\n166\t```\n167\t\n168\t### Example 3: Complex Input\n169\t\n170\t```go\n171\ttype ComplexInput struct {\n172\t    Name   string\n173\t    Count  int\n174\t    Active bool\n175\t}\n176\t\n177\tfunc TestComplexInput(t *testing.T) {\n178\t    table := []TableTestCase[ComplexInput, error]{\n179\t        {\n180\t            Name: \"valid input\",\n181\t            Input: ComplexInput{Name: \"test\", Count: 5, Active: true},\n182\t            ExpectError: false,\n183\t        },\n184\t        {\n185\t            Name: \"empty name\",\n186\t            Input: ComplexInput{Name: \"\", Count: 5, Active: true},\n187\t            ExpectError: true,\n188\t            ExpectedError: ErrEmptyName,\n189\t        },\n190\t        {\n191\t            Name: \"negative count\",\n192\t            Input: ComplexInput{Name: \"test\", Count: -1, Active: true},\n193\t            ExpectError: true,\n194\t            ExpectedError: ErrNegativeCount,\n195\t        },\n196\t    }\n197\t\n198\t    RunTable(t, table, ProcessComplexInput)\n199\t}\n200\t```\n201\t\n202\t### Example 4: Table Extension\n203\t\n204\t```go\n205\tfunc TestExtendedValidation(t *testing.T) {\n206\t    // Base test cases\n207\t    baseCases := []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n208\t        SuccessCase(\"valid 1\", \"test1\"),\n209\t        SuccessCase(\"valid 2\", \"test2\"),\n210\t        ErrorCase(\"invalid\", \"\", ErrEmpty),\n211\t    }\n212\t\n213\t    // Custom cases\n214\t    customCases := []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n215\t        {\n216\t            Name: \"custom case\",\n217\t            Input: \"custom\",\n218\t            ExpectError: false,\n219\t            Tags: []string{\"custom\"},\n220\t        },\n221\t    }\n222\t\n223\t    // Extend and run\n224\t    extended := ExtendTable(baseCases, customCases)\n225\t    RunTable(t, extended, ValidateInput)\n226\t}\n227\t```\n228\t\n229\t### Example 5: Filtered Tests\n230\t\n231\t```go\n232\tfunc TestFilteredValidation(t *testing.T) {\n233\t    allTests := []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n234\t        {Name: \"test 1\", Input: \"test1\", ExpectError: false, Tags: []string{\"smoke\"}},\n235\t        {Name: \"test 2\", Input: \"test2\", ExpectError: false, Tags: []string{\"regression\"}},\n236\t        {Name: \"test 3\", Input: \"test3\", ExpectError: false, Tags: []string{\"smoke\"}},\n237\t    }\n238\t\n239\t    // Run only smoke tests\n240\t    smokeTests := FilterTable(allTests, \"smoke\")\n241\t    RunTable(t, smokeTests, ValidateInput)\n242\t\n243\t    // Run only regression tests\n244\t    regressionTests := FilterTable(allTests, \"regression\")\n245\t    RunTable(t, regressionTests, ValidateInput)\n246\t}\n247\t```\n248\t\n249\t## Best Practices\n250\t\n251\t### 1. Use Descriptive Names\n252\t\n253\t```go\n254\t✓ Good: \"empty string returns error\"\n255\t✗ Bad: \"test 1\"\n256\t```\n257\t\n258\t### 2. Add Descriptions for Complex Tests\n259\t\n260\t```go\n261\t{\n262\t    Name: \"inactive with high count\",\n263\t    Description: \"Tests that inactive status with count > 5 returns error\",\n264\t    Input: ComplexInput{Active: false, Count: 10},\n265\t    ExpectError: true,\n266\t}\n267\t```\n268\t\n269\t### 3. Use Helper Functions When Appropriate\n270\t\n271\t```go\n272\t✓ Good: SuccessCase(\"valid input\", \"test\")\n273\t✓ Good: ErrorCase(\"invalid input\", \"\", ErrEmpty)\n274\t✗ Bad: Repetitive test case creation\n275\t```\n276\t\n277\t### 4. Use Tags for Filtering\n278\t\n279\t```go\n280\t{\n281\t    Name: \"critical test\",\n282\t    Input: \"input\",\n283\t    ExpectError: false,\n284\t    Tags: []string{\"smoke\", \"critical\", \"auth\"},\n285\t}\n286\t```\n287\t\n288\t### 5. Validate Error Messages\n289\t\n290\t```go\n291\t{\n292\t    Name: \"invalid input\",\n293\t    Input: \"invalid\",\n294\t    ExpectedError: ErrInvalid,\n295\t    ExpectError: true,\n296\t    ErrorContains: \"invalid format\", // Validate error message\n297\t}\n298\t```\n299\t\n300\t### 6. Organize Tests by Category\n301\t\n302\t```go\n303\tfunc TestMyFunction(t *testing.T) {\n304\t    t.Run(\"success cases\", func(t *testing.T) {\n305\t        table := []TableTestCase[string, error]{...}\n306\t        RunTable(t, table, MyFunction)\n307\t    })\n308\t\n309\t    t.Run(\"error cases\", func(t *testing.T) {\n310\t        table := []TableTestCase[string, error]{...}\n311\t        RunTable(t, table, MyFunction)\n312\t    })\n313\t}\n314\t```\n315\t\n316\t## Advanced Features\n317\t\n318\t### Custom Error Types\n319\t\n320\tThe framework works with any error type:\n321\t\n322\t```go\n323\ttype MyError struct {\n324\t    Code int\n325\t    Message string\n326\t}\n327\t\n328\tfunc (e MyError) Error() string {\n329\t    return fmt.Sprintf(\"%d: %s\", e.Code, e.Message)\n330\t}\n331\t\n332\tfunc TestCustomError(t *testing.T) {\n333\t    table := []TableTestCase[string, MyError]{\n334\t        {\n335\t            Name: \"returns custom error\",\n336\t            Input: \"invalid\",\n337\t            ExpectedError: MyError{Code: 400, Message: \"invalid\"},\n338\t            ExpectError: true,\n339\t        },\n340\t    }\n341\t\n342\t    RunTable(t, table, MyFunction)\n343\t}\n344\t```\n345\t\n346\t### Table-Level Organization\n347\t\n348\t```go\n349\tfunc TestComprehensiveValidation(t *testing.T) {\n350\t    tables := []struct {\n351\t        name string\n352\t        cases []TableTestCase[string, error]\n353\t    }{\n354\t        {\n355\t            name: \"empty input\",\n356\t            cases: []TableTestCase[string, error]{...},\n357\t        },\n358\t        {\n359\t            name: \"invalid format\",\n360\t            cases: []TableTestCase[string, error]{...},\n361\t        },\n362\t    }\n363\t\n364\t    for _, table := range tables {\n365\t        t.Run(table.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n366\t            RunTable(t, table.cases, ValidateInput)\n367\t        })\n368\t    }\n369\t}\n370\t```\n371\t\n372\t## Running Tests\n373\t\n374\t### Run All Tests\n375\t\n376\t```bash\n377\tgo test ./internal/testutil/...\n378\t```\n379\t\n380\t### Run Specific Test\n381\t\n382\t```bash\n383\tgo test -run TestBasicValidation ./internal/testutil/...\n384\t```\n385\t\n386\t### Run with Verbosity\n387\t\n388\t```bash\n389\tgo test -v ./internal/testutil/...\n390\t```\n391\t\n392\t## Comparison with Other Frameworks\n393\t\n394\t### vs. HTTP-Specific Frameworks\n395\t\n396\t- **Generic**: Works with any type, not just HTTP\n397\t- **Flexible**: No predefined response structure\n398\t- **Simple**: Fewer dependencies on HTTP types\n399\t\n400\t### vs. Basic Table-Driven Tests\n401\t\n402\t- **More structured**: Clear separation of test data and logic\n403\t- **Helper functions**: Convenience functions for common patterns\n404\t- **Filtering**: Built-in support for test filtering\n405\t- **Extension**: Easy to extend and manipulate tables\n406\t\n407\t## Request/Response Validation Helpers\n408\t\n409\tThe `validation_helpers.go` file provides comprehensive utilities for making test requests and validating HTTP responses in ARMOR testing.\n410\t\n411\t### Request Builders\n412\t\n413\t#### NewTestRequest\n414\t\n415\tCreates a basic test request:\n416\t\n417\t```go\n418\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n419\t```\n420\t\n421\t#### BuildTestRequest\n422\t\n423\tCreates a fully configured request from configuration:\n424\t\n425\t```go\n426\tcfg := TestRequestConfig{\n427\t    Method: \"GET\",\n428\t    Path:   \"/test-bucket/test-key\",\n429\t    Headers: map[string]string{\n430\t        \"X-Custom-Header\": \"value\",\n431\t    },\n432\t}\n433\treq := BuildTestRequest(cfg)\n434\t```\n435\t\n436\t#### With* Header Helpers\n437\t\n438\tAdd headers to requests:\n439\t\n440\t```go\n441\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n442\treq = WithAuthHeader(req, \"TESTACCESSKEY\", \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\")\n443\treq = WithDateHeader(req, time.Now())\n444\treq = WithHeader(req, \"X-Custom-Header\", \"value\")\n445\treq = WithExpiredDate(req) // Sets date to 20 minutes ago\n446\t```\n447\t\n448\t### Request Execution\n449\t\n450\t#### MakeRequest\n451\t\n452\tExecutes a request and captures response:\n453\t\n454\t```go\n455\thandler := server.Handler()\n456\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n457\tresp := MakeRequest(handler, req)\n458\t\n459\tassert.Equal(t, 403, resp.Code)\n460\t```\n461\t\n462\t#### MakeRequestWithTiming\n463\t\n464\tExecutes request with performance measurement:\n465\t\n466\t```go\n467\tresp := MakeRequestWithTiming(handler, req)\n468\tassert.Less(t, resp.ResponseTime, 100*time.Millisecond)\n469\t```\n470\t\n471\t### Response Validators\n472\t\n473\t#### ValidateStatusCode\n474\t\n475\t```go\n476\terr := ValidateStatusCode(resp, 403)\n477\tassert.NoError(t, err)\n478\t```\n479\t\n480\t#### ValidateContentType\n481\t\n482\t```go\n483\terr := ValidateContentType(resp, \"application/xml\")\n484\tassert.NoError(t, err)\n485\t```\n486\t\n487\t#### ValidateErrorCode\n488\t\n489\t```go\n490\terr := ValidateErrorCode(resp, \"MissingAuthenticationToken\")\n491\tassert.NoError(t, err)\n492\t```\n493\t\n494\t#### ValidateErrorMessage\n495\t\n496\t```go\n497\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(resp, \"authentication\")\n498\tassert.NoError(t, err)\n499\t```\n500\t\n501\t#### ValidateResponseTime\n502\t\n503\t```go\n504\terr := ValidateResponseTime(resp, 100*time.Millisecond)\n505\tassert.NoError(t, err)\n506\t```\n507\t\n508\t#### ValidateHeader\n509\t\n510\t```go\n511\terr := ValidateHeader(resp, \"X-Custom-Header\", \"expected-value\")\n512\tassert.NoError(t, err)\n513\t```\n514\t\n515\t### Comprehensive Assertions\n516\t\n517\t#### AssertErrorResponse\n518\t\n519\tValidates complete error response:\n520\t\n521\t```go\n522\tAssertErrorResponse(t, resp, \"MissingAuthenticationToken\", 403)\n523\t```\n524\t\n525\t#### AssertAuthenticationError\n526\t\n527\tValidates authentication error:\n528\t\n529\t```go\n530\tAssertAuthenticationError(t, resp)\n531\t```\n532\t\n533\t#### AssertAuthorizationError\n534\t\n535\tValidates authorization error:\n536\t\n537\t```go\n538\tAssertAuthorizationError(t, resp)\n539\t```\n540\t\n541\t#### Simple Assertions\n542\t\n543\t```go\n544\tAssertStatusCode(t, resp, 200)\n545\tAssertErrorType(t, resp, \"MissingAuthenticationToken\")\n546\tAssertErrorMessage(t, resp, \"authentication\")\n547\tAssertContentType(t, resp, \"application/xml\")\n548\tAssertResponseTimeUnder(t, resp, 100*time.Millisecond)\n549\t```\n550\t\n551\t### Complete Example\n552\t\n553\t```go\n554\tfunc TestMissingAuthentication(t *testing.T) {\n555\t    server := setupTestServer(t)\n556\t    handler := server.Handler()\n557\t\n558\t    // Create request\n559\t    req := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n560\t\n561\t    // Execute request with timing\n562\t    resp := MakeRequestWithTiming(handler, req)\n563\t\n564\t    // Validate error response\n565\t    AssertErrorResponse(t, resp.ResponseRecorder, \"MissingAuthenticationToken\", 403)\n566\t    AssertResponseTimeUnder(t, resp, 100*time.Millisecond)\n567\t    AssertContentType(t, resp.ResponseRecorder, \"application/xml\")\n568\t\n569\t    // Validate error message\n570\t    AssertErrorMessage(t, resp.ResponseRecorder, \"authentication\")\n571\t}\n572\t```\n573\t\n574\t### S3Error Type\n575\t\n576\tThe `S3Error` type represents ARMOR's XML error response format:\n577\t\n578\t```go\n579\ttype S3Error struct {\n580\t    XMLName xml.Name `xml:\"Error\"`\n581\t    Code    string   `xml:\"Code\"`\n582\t    Message string   `xml:\"Message\"`\n583\t}\n584\t```\n585\t\n586\tExample XML:\n587\t```xml\n588\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n589\t<Error>\n590\t    <Code>MissingAuthenticationToken</Code>\n591\t    <Message>The request must include authentication</Message>\n592\t</Error>\n593\t```\n594\t\n595\t### Error Codes\n596\t\n597\tARMOR uses standard S3 error codes:\n598\t\n599\t- `MissingAuthenticationToken`: Authorization header is missing\n600\t- `InvalidAccessKeyId`: The provided access key does not exist\n601\t- `SignatureDoesNotMatch`: Calculated signature does not match\n602\t- `RequestExpired`: Request timestamp is outside allowed window\n603\t- `AccessDenied`: ACL-based access control rejection\n604\t- `InvalidAlgorithm`: Only AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 is supported\n605\t- `IncompleteSignature`: Authorization header is missing required fields\n606\t- `MissingDateHeader`: X-Amz-Date header is missing\n607\t\n608\t### Performance Testing\n609\t\n610\tExample performance validation:\n611\t\n612\t```go\n613\tfunc TestErrorPerformance(t *testing.T) {\n614\t    handler := setupTestHandler(t)\n615\t\n616\t    req := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n617\t    resp := MakeRequestWithTiming(handler, req)\n618\t\n619\t    // Validate performance requirements\n620\t    AssertResponseTimeUnder(t, resp, 100*time.Millisecond)\n621\t\n622\t    // Also validate the error response\n623\t    AssertErrorResponse(t, resp.ResponseRecorder, \"MissingAuthenticationToken\", 403)\n624\t}\n625\t```\n626\t\n627\t## Contributing\n628\t\n629\tWhen adding new features to the test table framework:\n630\t\n631\t1. Maintain backward compatibility\n632\t2. Add comprehensive examples\n633\t3. Update this documentation\n634\t4. Add tests for new features\n635\t\n636\t## License\n637\t\n638\tPart of the ARMOR project.\n639\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"44f41e22-361b-40cc-951d-e41f9f487f4c","uuid":"7f5bcd43-2d9a-4f03-b302-399f61e447c0","timestamp":"2026-07-16T18:52:02.404Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/testutil/README.md","content":"# Generic Test Table Framework\n\n## Overview\n\nThe `testutil` package provides a comprehensive, generic test table framework for writing table-driven tests in Go. Unlike HTTP-specific test frameworks, this framework is designed to work with any type of test case, any input type, and any error type.\n\n## Features\n\n- ✅ **Generic**: Works with any input and error types\n- ✅ **Type-safe**: Strong typing prevents errors at compile time\n- ✅ **Reusable**: Test tables can be shared across multiple test files\n- ✅ **Extensible**: Easy to extend, filter, and manipulate test tables\n- ✅ **Convenient**: Helper functions for common test patterns\n- ✅ **Self-documenting**: Clear field names and comprehensive documentation\n\n## Quick Start\n\n### Basic Example\n\n```go\nfunc TestMyFunction(t *testing.T) {\n    // Define test table\n    table := []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n        {\n            Name:        \"empty string returns error\",\n            Description: \"Tests that empty string returns error\",\n            Input:       \"\",\n            ExpectedError: ErrEmpty,\n            ExpectError:  true,\n        },\n        {\n            Name:        \"valid string succeeds\",\n            Description: \"Tests that valid string succeeds\",\n            Input:       \"valid\",\n            ExpectedError: nil,\n            ExpectError:  false,\n        },\n    }\n\n    // Run test table\n    RunTable(t, table, func(input string) error {\n        return MyFunction(input)\n    })\n}\n```\n\n### Using Helper Functions\n\n```go\nfunc TestMyFunction(t *testing.T) {\n    table := []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n        SuccessCase(\"valid input\", \"test\"),\n        ErrorCase(\"invalid input\", \"\", ErrEmpty),\n        ErrorCaseWithMessage(\"format error\", \"invalid\", ErrInvalidFormat, \"invalid format\"),\n    }\n\n    RunTable(t, table, func(input string) error {\n        return MyFunction(input)\n    })\n}\n```\n\n### Using Table Builder\n\n```go\nfunc TestMyFunction(t *testing.T) {\n    table := NewTableBuilder[string, error]().\n        WithName(\"my tests\").\n        WithDescription(\"Test cases for my function\").\n        WithTags([]string{\"validation\"}).\n        WithTestCase(TableTestCase[string, error]{\n            Name: \"test case\",\n            Input: \"input\",\n            ExpectedError: nil,\n            ExpectError: false,\n        }).\n        Build()\n\n    RunTable(t, table.TestCases, func(input string) error {\n        return MyFunction(input)\n    })\n}\n```\n\n## Core Types\n\n### TableTestCase\n\nRepresents a single test case with the following fields:\n\n- `Name`: Test case name (required)\n- `Description`: Optional detailed description\n- `Input`: The input to test (any type)\n- `ExpectedError`: The expected error\n- `ExpectError`: Whether an error is expected\n- `ErrorContains`: Optional substring for error message validation\n- `ErrorIs`: Optional error for `errors.Is` validation\n- `Skip`: Whether to skip this test case\n- `Tags`: Optional tags for filtering\n\n### TableTestTable\n\nRepresents a collection of test cases:\n\n- `Name`: Table name\n- `Description`: Optional table description\n- `TestCases`: Collection of test cases\n- `Tags`: Optional tags for the entire table\n\n## Helper Functions\n\n### Test Case Creation\n\n- `SuccessCase(name, input)`: Creates a test case expecting success\n- `ErrorCase(name, input, error)`: Creates a test case expecting an error\n- `ErrorCaseWithMessage(name, input, error, message)`: Creates a test case expecting an error with message validation\n\n### Test Execution\n\n- `RunTable(t, table, runner)`: Executes all test cases in a table\n\n### Table Manipulation\n\n- `ExtendTable(base, custom)`: Extends a table with custom test cases\n- `FilterTable(table, tag)`: Filters a table by tag\n- `FilterTableByTag(table, excludeTag)`: Filters a table to exclude tests with a specific tag\n\n### Assertion Helpers\n\n- `AssertNoError(t, err, msg)`: Asserts that no error occurred\n- `AssertError(t, err, msg)`: Asserts that an error occurred\n- `AssertErrorIs(t, err, expected, msg)`: Asserts that the error matches the expected error\n- `AssertErrorContains(t, err, substring, msg)`: Asserts that the error message contains a substring\n\n## Examples\n\n### Example 1: String Validation\n\n```go\nfunc TestStringValidation(t *testing.T) {\n    table := []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n        SuccessCase(\"valid string\", \"test@example.com\"),\n        ErrorCase(\"empty string\", \"\", ErrEmpty),\n        ErrorCaseWithMessage(\"too short\", \"ab\", ErrTooShort, \"at least 3 characters\"),\n        ErrorCaseWithMessage(\"too long\", strings.Repeat(\"a\", 51), ErrTooLong, \"exceed 50\"),\n    }\n\n    RunTable(t, table, ValidateString)\n}\n```\n\n### Example 2: Numeric Validation\n\n```go\nfunc TestNumericValidation(t *testing.T) {\n    table := []TableTestCase[int, error]{\n        {Name: \"negative\", Input: -1, ExpectError: true, ErrorContains: \"non-negative\"},\n        {Name: \"zero\", Input: 0, ExpectError: false},\n        {Name: \"valid\", Input: 50, ExpectError: false},\n        {Name: \"maximum\", Input: 100, ExpectError: false},\n        {Name: \"exceeds max\", Input: 101, ExpectError: true, ErrorContains: \"exceed 100\"},\n    }\n\n    RunTable(t, table, ValidateNumber)\n}\n```\n\n### Example 3: Complex Input\n\n```go\ntype ComplexInput struct {\n    Name   string\n    Count  int\n    Active bool\n}\n\nfunc TestComplexInput(t *testing.T) {\n    table := []TableTestCase[ComplexInput, error]{\n        {\n            Name: \"valid input\",\n            Input: ComplexInput{Name: \"test\", Count: 5, Active: true},\n            ExpectError: false,\n        },\n        {\n            Name: \"empty name\",\n            Input: ComplexInput{Name: \"\", Count: 5, Active: true},\n            ExpectError: true,\n            ExpectedError: ErrEmptyName,\n        },\n        {\n            Name: \"negative count\",\n            Input: ComplexInput{Name: \"test\", Count: -1, Active: true},\n            ExpectError: true,\n            ExpectedError: ErrNegativeCount,\n        },\n    }\n\n    RunTable(t, table, ProcessComplexInput)\n}\n```\n\n### Example 4: Table Extension\n\n```go\nfunc TestExtendedValidation(t *testing.T) {\n    // Base test cases\n    baseCases := []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n        SuccessCase(\"valid 1\", \"test1\"),\n        SuccessCase(\"valid 2\", \"test2\"),\n        ErrorCase(\"invalid\", \"\", ErrEmpty),\n    }\n\n    // Custom cases\n    customCases := []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n        {\n            Name: \"custom case\",\n            Input: \"custom\",\n            ExpectError: false,\n            Tags: []string{\"custom\"},\n        },\n    }\n\n    // Extend and run\n    extended := ExtendTable(baseCases, customCases)\n    RunTable(t, extended, ValidateInput)\n}\n```\n\n### Example 5: Filtered Tests\n\n```go\nfunc TestFilteredValidation(t *testing.T) {\n    allTests := []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n        {Name: \"test 1\", Input: \"test1\", ExpectError: false, Tags: []string{\"smoke\"}},\n        {Name: \"test 2\", Input: \"test2\", ExpectError: false, Tags: []string{\"regression\"}},\n        {Name: \"test 3\", Input: \"test3\", ExpectError: false, Tags: []string{\"smoke\"}},\n    }\n\n    // Run only smoke tests\n    smokeTests := FilterTable(allTests, \"smoke\")\n    RunTable(t, smokeTests, ValidateInput)\n\n    // Run only regression tests\n    regressionTests := FilterTable(allTests, \"regression\")\n    RunTable(t, regressionTests, ValidateInput)\n}\n```\n\n## Best Practices\n\n### 1. Use Descriptive Names\n\n```go\n✓ Good: \"empty string returns error\"\n✗ Bad: \"test 1\"\n```\n\n### 2. Add Descriptions for Complex Tests\n\n```go\n{\n    Name: \"inactive with high count\",\n    Description: \"Tests that inactive status with count > 5 returns error\",\n    Input: ComplexInput{Active: false, Count: 10},\n    ExpectError: true,\n}\n```\n\n### 3. Use Helper Functions When Appropriate\n\n```go\n✓ Good: SuccessCase(\"valid input\", \"test\")\n✓ Good: ErrorCase(\"invalid input\", \"\", ErrEmpty)\n✗ Bad: Repetitive test case creation\n```\n\n### 4. Use Tags for Filtering\n\n```go\n{\n    Name: \"critical test\",\n    Input: \"input\",\n    ExpectError: false,\n    Tags: []string{\"smoke\", \"critical\", \"auth\"},\n}\n```\n\n### 5. Validate Error Messages\n\n```go\n{\n    Name: \"invalid input\",\n    Input: \"invalid\",\n    ExpectedError: ErrInvalid,\n    ExpectError: true,\n    ErrorContains: \"invalid format\", // Validate error message\n}\n```\n\n### 6. Organize Tests by Category\n\n```go\nfunc TestMyFunction(t *testing.T) {\n    t.Run(\"success cases\", func(t *testing.T) {\n        table := []TableTestCase[string, error]{...}\n        RunTable(t, table, MyFunction)\n    })\n\n    t.Run(\"error cases\", func(t *testing.T) {\n        table := []TableTestCase[string, error]{...}\n        RunTable(t, table, MyFunction)\n    })\n}\n```\n\n## Advanced Features\n\n### Custom Error Types\n\nThe framework works with any error type:\n\n```go\ntype MyError struct {\n    Code int\n    Message string\n}\n\nfunc (e MyError) Error() string {\n    return fmt.Sprintf(\"%d: %s\", e.Code, e.Message)\n}\n\nfunc TestCustomError(t *testing.T) {\n    table := []TableTestCase[string, MyError]{\n        {\n            Name: \"returns custom error\",\n            Input: \"invalid\",\n            ExpectedError: MyError{Code: 400, Message: \"invalid\"},\n            ExpectError: true,\n        },\n    }\n\n    RunTable(t, table, MyFunction)\n}\n```\n\n### Table-Level Organization\n\n```go\nfunc TestComprehensiveValidation(t *testing.T) {\n    tables := []struct {\n        name string\n        cases []TableTestCase[string, error]\n    }{\n        {\n            name: \"empty input\",\n            cases: []TableTestCase[string, error]{...},\n        },\n        {\n            name: \"invalid format\",\n            cases: []TableTestCase[string, error]{...},\n        },\n    }\n\n    for _, table := range tables {\n        t.Run(table.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n            RunTable(t, table.cases, ValidateInput)\n        })\n    }\n}\n```\n\n## Running Tests\n\n### Run All Tests\n\n```bash\ngo test ./internal/testutil/...\n```\n\n### Run Specific Test\n\n```bash\ngo test -run TestBasicValidation ./internal/testutil/...\n```\n\n### Run with Verbosity\n\n```bash\ngo test -v ./internal/testutil/...\n```\n\n## Comparison with Other Frameworks\n\n### vs. HTTP-Specific Frameworks\n\n- **Generic**: Works with any type, not just HTTP\n- **Flexible**: No predefined response structure\n- **Simple**: Fewer dependencies on HTTP types\n\n### vs. Basic Table-Driven Tests\n\n- **More structured**: Clear separation of test data and logic\n- **Helper functions**: Convenience functions for common patterns\n- **Filtering**: Built-in support for test filtering\n- **Extension**: Easy to extend and manipulate tables\n\n## Request/Response Validation Helpers\n\nThe `validation_helpers.go` file provides comprehensive utilities for making test requests and validating HTTP responses in ARMOR testing.\n\n### Request Builders\n\n#### NewTestRequest\n\nCreates a basic test request:\n\n```go\nreq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n```\n\n#### BuildTestRequest\n\nCreates a fully configured request from configuration:\n\n```go\ncfg := TestRequestConfig{\n    Method: \"GET\",\n    Path:   \"/test-bucket/test-key\",\n    Headers: map[string]string{\n        \"X-Custom-Header\": \"value\",\n    },\n}\nreq := BuildTestRequest(cfg)\n```\n\n#### With* Header Helpers\n\nAdd headers to requests:\n\n```go\nreq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\nreq = WithAuthHeader(req, \"TESTACCESSKEY\", \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\")\nreq = WithDateHeader(req, time.Now())\nreq = WithHeader(req, \"X-Custom-Header\", \"value\")\nreq = WithExpiredDate(req) // Sets date to 20 minutes ago\n```\n\n### Request Execution\n\n#### MakeRequest\n\nExecutes a request and captures response:\n\n```go\nhandler := server.Handler()\nreq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\nresp := MakeRequest(handler, req)\n\nassert.Equal(t, 403, resp.Code)\n```\n\n#### MakeRequestWithTiming\n\nExecutes request with performance measurement:\n\n```go\nresp := MakeRequestWithTiming(handler, req)\nassert.Less(t, resp.ResponseTime, 100*time.Millisecond)\n```\n\n### Response Validators\n\n#### ValidateStatusCode\n\n```go\nerr := ValidateStatusCode(resp, 403)\nassert.NoError(t, err)\n```\n\n#### ValidateContentType\n\n```go\nerr := ValidateContentType(resp, \"application/xml\")\nassert.NoError(t, err)\n```\n\n#### ValidateErrorCode\n\n```go\nerr := ValidateErrorCode(resp, \"MissingAuthenticationToken\")\nassert.NoError(t, err)\n```\n\n#### ValidateErrorMessage\n\n```go\nerr := ValidateErrorMessage(resp, \"authentication\")\nassert.NoError(t, err)\n```\n\n#### ValidateResponseTime\n\n```go\nerr := ValidateResponseTime(resp, 100*time.Millisecond)\nassert.NoError(t, err)\n```\n\n#### ValidateHeader\n\n```go\nerr := ValidateHeader(resp, \"X-Custom-Header\", \"expected-value\")\nassert.NoError(t, err)\n```\n\n### Comprehensive Assertions\n\n#### AssertErrorResponse\n\nValidates complete error response:\n\n```go\nAssertErrorResponse(t, resp, \"MissingAuthenticationToken\", 403)\n```\n\n#### AssertAuthenticationError\n\nValidates authentication error:\n\n```go\nAssertAuthenticationError(t, resp)\n```\n\n#### AssertAuthorizationError\n\nValidates authorization error:\n\n```go\nAssertAuthorizationError(t, resp)\n```\n\n#### Simple Assertions\n\n```go\nAssertStatusCode(t, resp, 200)\nAssertErrorType(t, resp, \"MissingAuthenticationToken\")\nAssertErrorMessage(t, resp, \"authentication\")\nAssertContentType(t, resp, \"application/xml\")\nAssertResponseTimeUnder(t, resp, 100*time.Millisecond)\n```\n\n### Complete Example\n\n```go\nfunc TestMissingAuthentication(t *testing.T) {\n    server := setupTestServer(t)\n    handler := server.Handler()\n\n    // Create request\n    req := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n\n    // Execute request with timing\n    resp := MakeRequestWithTiming(handler, req)\n\n    // Validate error response\n    AssertErrorResponse(t, resp.ResponseRecorder, \"MissingAuthenticationToken\", 403)\n    AssertResponseTimeUnder(t, resp, 100*time.Millisecond)\n    AssertContentType(t, resp.ResponseRecorder, \"application/xml\")\n\n    // Validate error message\n    AssertErrorMessage(t, resp.ResponseRecorder, \"authentication\")\n}\n```\n\n### S3Error Type\n\nThe `S3Error` type represents ARMOR's XML error response format:\n\n```go\ntype S3Error struct {\n    XMLName xml.Name `xml:\"Error\"`\n    Code    string   `xml:\"Code\"`\n    Message string   `xml:\"Message\"`\n}\n```\n\nExample XML:\n```xml\n<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n    <Code>MissingAuthenticationToken</Code>\n    <Message>The request must include authentication</Message>\n</Error>\n```\n\n### Error Codes\n\nARMOR uses standard S3 error codes:\n\n- `MissingAuthenticationToken`: Authorization header is missing\n- `InvalidAccessKeyId`: The provided access key does not exist\n- `SignatureDoesNotMatch`: Calculated signature does not match\n- `RequestExpired`: Request timestamp is outside allowed window\n- `AccessDenied`: ACL-based access control rejection\n- `InvalidAlgorithm`: Only AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 is supported\n- `IncompleteSignature`: Authorization header is missing required fields\n- `MissingDateHeader`: X-Amz-Date header is missing\n\n### Performance Testing\n\nExample performance validation:\n\n```go\nfunc TestErrorPerformance(t *testing.T) {\n    handler := setupTestHandler(t)\n\n    req := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n    resp := MakeRequestWithTiming(handler, req)\n\n    // Validate performance requirements\n    AssertResponseTimeUnder(t, resp, 100*time.Millisecond)\n\n    // Also validate the error response\n    AssertErrorResponse(t, resp.ResponseRecorder, \"MissingAuthenticationToken\", 403)\n}\n```\n\n## Contributing\n\nWhen adding new features to the test table framework:\n\n1. Maintain backward compatibility\n2. Add comprehensive examples\n3. Update this documentation\n4. Add tests for new features\n\n## License\n\nPart of the ARMOR project.\n","numLines":639,"startLine":1,"totalLines":639}}}

Use the TestRequestBuilder functions to construct\n71\t// requests incrementally.\n72\t//\n73\t// Example:\n74\t//\n75\t//\tcfg := TestRequestConfig{\n76\t//\t    Method: \"GET\",\n77\t//\t    Path: \"/test-bucket/test-key\",\n78\t//\t}\n79\t//\treq := BuildTestRequest(cfg)\n80\t//\treq = WithAuthHeader(req, \"test-key\", \"test-secret\")\n81\ttype TestRequestConfig struct {\n82\t\tMethod      string\n83\t\tPath        string\n84\t\tBody        []byte\n85\t\tHeaders     map[string]string\n86\t\tQueryParams map[string]string\n87\t}\n88\t\n89\t// NewTestRequest creates a basic test request with method and path.\n90\t//\n91\t// This is the simplest way to create a test request. Additional headers\n92\t// and parameters can be added using the With* helper functions.\n93\t//\n94\t// Parameters:\n95\t//   - method: HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, etc.)\n96\t//   - path: Request path (e.g., \"/test-bucket/test-key\")\n97\t//   - body: Optional request body\n98\t//\n99\t// Returns:\n100\t//   - *http.Request: Configured test request\n101\t//\n102\t// Example:\n103\t//\n104\t//\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n105\t//\treq = WithAuthHeader(req, \"test-key\", \"test-secret\")\n106\tfunc NewTestRequest(method, path string, body []byte) *http.Request {\n107\t\treturn httptest.NewRequest(method, path, nil)\n108\t}\n109\t\n110\t// BuildTestRequest creates a test request from configuration.\n111\t//\n112\t// This function builds a complete test request from a TestRequestConfig,\n113\t// including all headers and query parameters.\n114\t//\n115\t// Parameters:\n116\t//   - config: TestRequestConfig with all request parameters\n117\t//\n118\t// Returns:\n119\t//   - *http.Request: Fully configured test request\n120\t//\n121\t// Example:\n122\t//\n123\t//\tcfg := TestRequestConfig{\n124\t//\t    Method: \"GET\",\n125\t//\t    Path: \"/test-bucket/test-key\",\n126\t//\t    Headers: map[string]string{\n127\t//\t        \"X-Custom-Header\": \"value\",\n128\t//\t    },\n129\t//\t    QueryParams: map[string]string{\n130\t//\t        \"versioning\": \"false\",\n131\t//\t    },\n132\t//\t}\n133\t//\treq := BuildTestRequest(cfg)\n134\tfunc BuildTestRequest(config TestRequestConfig) *http.Request {\n135\t\tvar path string\n136\t\tif config.QueryParams != nil && len(config.QueryParams) > 0 {\n137\t\t\tpath = config.Path + \"?\" + encodeQueryParams(config.QueryParams)\n138\t\t} else {\n139\t\t\tpath = config.Path\n140\t\t}\n141\t\n142\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(config.Method, path, nil)\n143\t\tif config.Body != nil {\n144\t\t\treq.Body = nil // Would need to set up properly\n145\t\t}\n146\t\n147\t\tfor key, value := range config.Headers {\n148\t\t\treq.Header.Set(key, value)\n149\t\t}\n150\t\n151\t\treturn req\n152\t}\n153\t\n154\t// WithAuthHeader adds auth header (actual\n158\t// signature validation depends on the test server implementation).\n159\t//\n160\t// Parameters:\n161\t//   - req: The request to modify\n162\t//   - accessKey: AWS access key ID\n163\t//   - secretKey: AWS secret access key\n164\t//\n165\t// Returns:\n166\t//   - *http.Request: The modified request\n167\t//\n168\t// Example:\n169\t//\n170\t//\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n171\t//\treq = WithAuthHeader(req, \"TESTACCESSKEY\", \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\")\n172\tfunc WithAuthHeader(req *http.Request, accessKey, secretKey string) *http.Request {\n173\t\ttimestamp := time.Now().Format(\"20060102T150405Z\")\n174\t\tcredential := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request\", accessKey)\n175\t\tsignature := \"test-signature\" // Simplified for testing\n176\t\n177\t\tauthHeader := fmt.Sprintf(\"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=%s, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=%s\",\n178\t\t\tcredential, signature)\n179\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", authHeader)\n180\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", timestamp)\n181\t\n182\t\treturn req\n183\t}\n184\t\n185\t// WithDateHeader adds X-Amz-Date header to request.\n186\t//\n187\t// This helper adds the AWS-standard date header used for request validation.\n188\t//\n189\t// Parameters:\n190\t//   - req: The request to modify\n191\t//   - t: Time for the date header\n192\t//\n193\t// Returns:\n194\t//   - *http.Request: The modified request\n195\t//\n196\t// Example:\n197\t//\n198\t//\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n199\t//\treq = WithDateHeader(req, time.Now())\n200\tfunc WithDateHeader(req *http.Request, t time.Time) *http.Request {\n201\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", t.Format(\"20060102T150405Z\"))\n202\t\treturn req\n203\t}\n204\t\n205\t// WithHeader adds a custom header to request.\n206\t//\n207\t// This is a generic helper for adding any custom header to a test request.\n208\t//\n209\t// Parameters:\n210\t//   - req: The request to modify\n211\t//   - key: Header name\n212\t//   - value: Header value)\n223\t\treturn req\n224\t}\n225\t\n226\t// WithExpiredDate sets the date header to an expired timestamp.\n227\t//\n228\t// This helper creates a request with a date header that is outside the\n229\t// allowed time window for testing expiration scenarios.\n230\t//\n231\t// Parameters:\n232\t//   - req: The request to modify\n233\t//\n234\t// Returns:\n235\t//   - *http.Request: The modified request\n236\t//\n237\t// Example:\n238\t//\n239\t//\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n240\t//\treq = WithExpiredDate(req) // Date will be 20 minutes in the past\n241\tfunc WithExpiredDate(req *http.Request) *http.Request {\n242\t\toldTime := time.Now().Add(-20 * time.Minute)\n243\t\treturn WithDateHeader(req, oldTime)\n244\t}\n245\t\n246\t// =============================================================================\n247\t// REQUEST EXECUTION\n248\t// =============================================================================\n249\t// These helpers execute test requests and capture responses.\n250\t// =============================================================================\n251\t\n252\t// MakeRequest executes a test request against a handler.\n253\t//\n254\t// This is a convenience function for executing requests and capturing responses.\n255\t// It uses httptest.NewRecorder() to capture the response.\n256\t//\n257\t// Parameters:\n258\t//   - handler: The HTTP handler to test\n259\t//   - req: The request to execute\n260\t//\n261\t// Returns:\n262\t//   - *httptest.ResponseRecorder: Response recorder with response data\n263\t//\n264\t// Example:\n265\t//\n266\t//\tserver := setupTestServer(t)\n267\t//\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n268\t//\tresp := MakeRequest(server.Handler(), req)\n269\t//\n270\t//\tassert.Equal(t, 403, resp.Code)\n271\tfunc MakeRequest(handler http.Handler, req *http.Request) *httptest.ResponseRecorder {\n272\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n273\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n274\t\treturn w\n275\t}\n276\t\n277\t// MakeRequestWithTiming executes a request and measures response time.\n278\t//\n279\t// This function executes a request while measuring the time taken to respond.\n280\t// The response time is included in the TestResponse struct for performance\n281\t// validation.\n282\t//\n283\t// Parameters:\n284\t//   - handler: The HTTP handler to test\n285\t//   - req: The request to execute\n286\t//\n287\t// Returns:\n288\t//   - *TestResponse: Response with timing information\n289\t//\n290\t// Example:\n291\t//\n292\t//\tserver := setupTestServer(t)\n293\t//\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n294\t//\tresp := MakeRequestWithTiming(server.Handler(), req)\n295\t//\n296\t//\tassert.Less(t, resp.ResponseTime, 100*time.Millisecond)\n297\tfunc MakeRequestWithTiming(handler http.Handler, req *http.Request) *TestResponse {\n298\t\tstart := time.Now()\n299\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n300\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n301\t\telapsed := time.Since(start)\n302\t\n303\t\treturn &TestResponse{\n304\t\t\tResponseRecorder: w,\n305\t\t\tResponseTime:     elapsed,\n306\t\t}\n307\t}\n308\t\n309\t// =============================================================================\n310\t// RESPONSE TYPES\n311\t// =============================================================================\n312\t// These types encapsulate response data for validation.\n313\t// =============================================================================\n314\t\n315\t// S3Error represents the standard S3 XML error response format.\n316\t//\n317\t// This type is used to unmarshal XML error responses from ARMOR,\n318\t// which follows the S3 error response specification.\n319\t//\n320\t// Example XML:\n321\t//\n322\t//\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n323\t//\t<Error>\n324\t//\t    <Code>MissingAuthenticationToken</Code>\n325\t//\t    <Message>The request must include either a valid (registered) AWS Access Key Id or a valid X.509 certificate</Message>\n326\t//\t</Error>\n327\ttype S3Error struct {\n328\t\tXMLName xml.Name `xml:\"Error\"`\n329\t\tCode    string   `xml:\"Code\"`\n330\t\tMessage string   `xml:\"Message\"`\n331\t}\n332\t\n333\t// TestResponse wraps an HTTP response with additional validation metadata.\n334\t//\n335\t// This type extends httptest.ResponseRecorder with additional information\n336\t// useful for testing, such as response time and parsed error data.\n337\t//\n338\t// Fields:\n339\t//   - ResponseRecorder: The underlying response recorder\n340\t//   - ResponseTime: Time taken to receive the response\n341\t//   - S3Error: Parsed S3 error (if response is an error)\n342\ttype TestResponse struct {\n343\t\tResponseRecorder *httptest.ResponseRecorder\n344\t\tResponseTime     time.Duration\n345\t\tS3Error          *S3Error\n346\t}\n347\t\n348\t// =============================================================================\n349\t// RESPONSE VALIDATORS\n350\t// =============================================================================\n351\t// These validators check specific aspects of HTTP responses.\n352\t// =============================================================================\n353\t\n354\t// ValidateStatusCode checks if response has expected status code.\n355\t//\n356\t// This validator checks the HTTP status code and returns an error if it\n357\t// doesn't match the expected value.\n358\t//\n359\t// Parameters:\n360\t//   - resp: Response recorder from request\n361\t//   - expectedCode: Expected HTTP status code\n362\t//\n363\t// Returns:\n364\t//   - error: nil if status code matches, error otherwise\n365\t//\n366\t// Example:\n367\t//\n368\t//\tresp := MakeRequest(handler, req)\n369\t//\terr := ValidateStatusCode(resp, 403)\n370\t//\tassert.NoError(t, err)\n371\tfunc ValidateStatusCode(resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedCode int) error {\n372\t\tif resp.Code != expectedCode {\n373\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected status code %d, got %d\", expectedCode, resp.Code)\n374\t\t}\n375\t\treturn nil\n376\t}\n377\t\n378\t// ValidateContentType checks if response has expected content type.\n379\t//\n380\t// This validator checks the Content-Type header and returns an error if it\n381\t// doesn't match the expected value.\n382\t//\n383\t// Parameters:\n384\t//   - resp: Response recorder from request\n385\t//   - expectedContentType: Expected content type (e.g., \"application/xml\")\n386\t//\n387\t// Returns:\n388\t//   - error: nil if content type matches, error otherwise\n389\t//\n390\t// Example:\n391\t//\n392\t//\tresp := MakeRequest(handler, req)\n393\t//\terr := ValidateContentType(resp, \"application/xml\")\n394\t//\tassert.NoError(t, err)\n395\tfunc ValidateContentType(resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedContentType string) error {\n396\t\tcontentType := resp.Header().Get(\"Content-Type\")\n397\t\tif contentType != expectedContentType {\n398\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected Content-Type '%s', got '%s'\", expectedContentType, contentType)\n399\t\t}\n400\t\treturn nil\n401\t}\n402\t\n403\t// ValidateErrorCode checks if response contains expected S3 error code.\n404\t//\n405\t// This validator parses the XML error response and checks if the error code\n406\t// matches the expected value.\n407\t//\n408\t// Parameters:\n409\t//   - resp: Response recorder from request\n410\t//   - expectedCode: Expected S3 error code (e.g., \"MissingAuthenticationToken\")\n411\t//\n412\t// Returns:\n413\t//   - error: nil if error code matches, error otherwise\n414\t//\n415\t// Example:\n416\t//\n417\t//\tresp := MakeRequest(handler, req)\n418\t//\terr := ValidateErrorCode(resp, \"MissingAuthenticationToken\")\n419\t//\tassert.NoError(t, err)\n420\tfunc ValidateErrorCode(resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedCode string) error {\n421\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n422\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(resp.Body.Bytes(), &s3Err); err != nil {\n423\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse error response: %w\", err)\n424\t\t}\n425\t\n426\t\tif s3Err.Code != expectedCode {\n427\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected error code '%s', got '%s'\", expectedCode, s3Err.Code)\n428\t\t}\n429\t\treturn nil\n430\t}\n431\t\n432\t// ValidateErrorMessage checks if error message contains expected substring.\n433\t//\n434\t// This validator parses the XML error response and checks if the error message\n435\t// contains the expected substring.\n436\t//\n437\t// Parameters:\n438\t//   - resp: Response recorder from request\n439\t//   - expectedSubstring: Substring that should be in the error message\n440\t//\n441\t// Returns:\n442\t//   - error: nil if message contains substring, error otherwise\n443\t//\n444\t// Example:\n445\t//\n446\t//\tresp := MakeRequest(handler, req)\n447\t//\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(resp, \"missing authentication\")\n448\t//\tassert.NoError(t, err)\n449\tfunc ValidateErrorMessage(resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedSubstring string) error {\n450\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n451\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(resp.Body.Bytes(), &s3Err); err != nil {\n452\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse error response: %w\", err)\n453\t\t}\n454\t\n455\t\tif !strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(s3Err.Message), strings.ToLower(expectedSubstring)) {\n456\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected error message to contain '%s', got '%s'\", expectedSubstring, s3Err.Message)\n457\t\t}\n458\t\treturn nil\n459\t}\n460\t\n461\t// ValidateResponseTime checks if response completed within time limit.\n462\t//\n463\t// This validator checks if the response time is within the specified threshold.\n464\t// Useful for performance testing and ensuring error responses are fast.\n465\t//\n466\t// Parameters:\n467\t//   - resp: Test response with timing information\n468\t//   - maxDuration: Maximum allowed response duration\n469\t//\n470\t// Returns:\n471\t//   - error: nil if response time is under threshold, error otherwise\n472\t//\n473\t// Example:\n474\t//\n475\t//\tresp := MakeRequestWithTiming(handler, req)\n476\t//\terr := ValidateResponseTime(resp, 100*time.Millisecond)\n477\t//\tassert.NoError(t, err)\n478\tfunc ValidateResponseTime(resp *TestResponse, maxDuration time.Duration) error {\n479\t\tif resp.ResponseTime > maxDuration {\n480\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"response time %v exceeds maximum %v\", resp.ResponseTime, maxDuration)\n481\t\t}\n482\t\treturn nil\n483\t}\n484\t\n485\t// ValidateHeader checks if response has expected header value.\n486\t//\n487\t// This validator checks if a specific response header matches the expected value.\n488\t//\n489\t// Parameters:\n490\t//   - resp: Response recorder from request\n491\t//   - header: Header name to check\n492\t//   - expectedValue: Expected header value\n493\t//\n494\t// Returns:\n495\t//   - error: nil if header matches, error otherwise\n496\t//\n497\t// Example:\n498\t//\n499\t//\tresp := MakeRequest(handler, req)\n500\t//\terr := ValidateHeader(resp, \"X-Custom-Header\", \"expected-value\")\n501\t//\tassert.NoError(t, err)\n502\tfunc ValidateHeader(resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder, header, expectedValue string) error {\n503\t\tvalue := resp.Header().Get(header)\n504\t\tif value != expectedValue {\n505\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected header '%s' to be '%s', got '%s'\", header, expectedValue, value)\n506\t\t}\n507\t\treturn nil\n508\t}\n509\t\n510\t// =============================================================================\n511\t// COMPREHENSIVE ASSERTIONS\n512\t// =============================================================================\n513\t// These assertions combine multiple validators for common test scenarios.\n514\t// =============================================================================\n515\t\n516\t// AssertErrorResponse validates complete error response.\n517\t//\n518\t// This assertion validates that the response is an error response with the\n519\t// specified error code and HTTP status code. It combines multiple validators\n520\t// for comprehensive error response checking.\n521\t//\n522\t// Parameters:\n523\t//   - t: Testing instance for reporting failures\n524\t//   - resp: Response recorder from request\n525\t//   - expectedErrorCode: Expected S3 error code\n526\t//   - expectedStatusCode: Expected HTTP status code\n527\t//\n528\t// Example:\n529\t//\n530\t//\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n531\t//\tresp := MakeRequest(handler, req)\n532\t//\tAssertErrorResponse(t, resp, \"MissingAuthenticationToken\", 403)\n533\tfunc AssertErrorResponse(t *testing.T, resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedErrorCode string, expectedStatusCode int) {\n534\t\tt.Helper()\n535\t\n536\t\tif err := ValidateStatusCode(resp, expectedStatusCode); err != nil {\n537\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Status code validation failed: %v\", err)\n538\t\t}\n539\t\n540\t\tif err := ValidateErrorCode(resp, expectedErrorCode); err != nil {\n541\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error code validation failed: %v\", err)\n542\t\t}\n543\t\n544\t\tif err := ValidateContentType(resp, \"application/xml\"); err != nil {\n545\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Content-Type validation failed: %v\", err)\n546\t\t}\n547\t}\n548\t\n549\t// AssertAuthenticationError validates authentication error response.\n550\t//\n551\t// This assertion checks for missing or invalid authentication errors.\n552\t// It validates that the response indicates an authentication problem.\n553\t//\n554\t// Parameters:\n555\t//   - t: Testing instance for reporting failures\n556\t//   - resp: Response recorder from request\n557\t//\n558\t// Example:\n559\t//\n560\t//\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n561\t//\tresp := MakeRequest(handler, req)\n562\t//\tAssertAuthenticationError(t, resp)\n563\tfunc AssertAuthenticationError(t *testing.T, resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder) {\n564\t\tt.Helper()\n565\t\n566\t\t// Check for authentication-related error codes\n567\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n568\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(resp.Body.Bytes(), &s3Err); err != nil {\n569\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n570\t\t}\n571\t\n572\t\tauthErrorCodes := map[string]bool{\n573\t\t\t\"MissingAuthenticationToken\": true,\n574\t\t\t\"InvalidAccessKeyId\":          true,\n575\t\t\t\"SignatureDoesNotMatch\":       true,\n576\t\t\t\"IncompleteSignature\":         true,\n577\t\t\t\"InvalidAlgorithm\":            true,\n578\t\t}\n579\t\n580\t\tif !authErrorCodes[s3Err.Code] {\n581\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected authentication error code, got '%s'\", s3Err.Code)\n582\t\t}\n583\t\n584\t\tif resp.Code != 403 {\n585\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected status 403 for authentication error, got %d\", resp.Code)\n586\t\t}\n587\t}\n588\t\n589\t// AssertAuthorizationError validates authorization error response.\n590\t//\n591\t// This assertion checks for permission/authorization errors.\n592\t// It validates that the response indicates an authorization problem.\n593\t//\n594\t// Parameters:\n595\t//   - t: Testing instance for reporting failures\n596\t//   - resp: Response recorder from request\n597\t//\n598\t// Example:\n599\t//\n600\t//\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n601\t//\treq = WithAuthHeader(req, \"RESTRICTEDKEY\", \"secret\")\n602\t//\tresp := MakeRequest(handler, req)\n603\t//\tAssertAuthorizationError(t, resp)\n604\tfunc AssertAuthorizationError(t *testing.T, resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder) {\n605\t\tt.Helper()\n606\t\n607\t\t// Check for authorization-related error codes\n608\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n609\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(resp.Body.Bytes(), &s3Err); err != nil {\n610\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n611\t\t}\n612\t\n613\t\tauthzErrorCodes := map[string]bool{\n614\t\t\t\"AccessDenied\":      true,\n615\t\t\t\"RequestExpired\":    true,\n616\t\t\t\"MissingDateHeader\": true,\n617\t\t}\n618\t\n619\t\tif !authzErrorCodes[s3Err.Code] {\n620\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected authorization error code, got '%s'\", s3Err.Code)\n621\t\t}\n622\t\n623\t\tif resp.Code != 403 {\n624\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected status 403 for authorization error, got %d\", resp.Code)\n625\t\t}\n626\t}\n627\t\n628\t// AssertStatusCode validates response status code.\n629\t//\n630\t// This is a simple assertion for status code validation.\n631\t//\n632\t// Parameters:\n633\t//   - t: Testing instance for reporting failures\n634\t//   - resp: Response recorder from request\n635\t//   - expectedCode: Expected HTTP status code\n636\t//\n637\t// Example:\n638\t//\n639\t//\tresp := MakeRequest(handler, req)\n640\t//\tAssertStatusCode(t, resp, 200)\n641\tfunc AssertStatusCode(t *testing.T, resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedCode int) {\n642\t\tt.Helper()\n643\t\tif err := ValidateStatusCode(resp, expectedCode); err != nil {\n745\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Response time validation failed: %v\", err)\n746\t\t}\n747\t}\n748\t\n749\t// =============================================================================\n750\t// UTILITY FUNCTIONS\n751\t// =============================================================================\n752\t// These functions provide common utilities for test helpers.\n753\t// =============================================================================\n754\t\n755\t// encodeQueryParams encodes query parameters for URL.\n756\t//\n757\t// This is a simple utility function for encoding query parameters.\n758\tfunc encodeQueryParams(params map[string]string) string {\n759\t\tvar parts []string\n760\t\tfor key, value := range params {\n761\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s=%s\", key, value))\n762\t\t}\n763\t\treturn strings.Join(parts, \"&\")\n764\t}\n765\t\n766\t// ParseS3Error parses S3 error from response body.\n767\t//\n768\t// This utility function parses the XML error response and returns the\n769\t// parsed S3Error struct. Returns error if parsing fails.\n770\t//\n771\t// Parameters:\n772\t//   - body: Response body bytes\n773\t//\n774\t// Returns:\n775\t//   - *S3Error: Parsed error response\n776\t//   - error: Parsing error if any\n777\t//\n778\t// Example:\n779\t//\n780\t//\tvar s3Err S3Error\n781\t//\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(resp.Body.Bytes(), &s3Err);
